page: OVHcloud a10-45 is a KVM-based GPU-accelerated server featuring an AMD EPYC 9554 processor with 30 dedicated physical cores running at 3.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 42.0 GB of system memory, yielding 1.4 GB per core, and includes a 400 GB local SSD. Graphics and parallel computing acceleration are provided by a single NVIDIA A10 GPU based on the Ampere architecture, offering 24 GB of dedicated VRAM. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core scores in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Multi-core capabilities are strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests. Memory performance is mixed; small-block cached read bandwidth is strong, while large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier. The server demonstrates exceptional performance in LLM inference for small and medium models, achieving top-tier token generation and prompt processing speeds. However, a notable tradeoff is observed with large models, where text generation speed drops to a poor rating due to the 24 GB VRAM limitation.

From a resource density perspective, the a10-45 offers a highly specialized hardware profile that balances dedicated AMD EPYC compute cores with a mid-range enterprise GPU. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require dedicated CPU execution alongside GPU acceleration without the overhead of massive system memory. It is particularly well-suited for medium-scale LLM inference, machine learning model training, 3D rendering, and parallel data processing. It is less optimal for memory-intensive database operations or very large language models that exceed the onboard VRAM capacity.
description: The OVHcloud a10-45 is a KVM-virtualized server designed for GPU-accelerated and compute-intensive workloads. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9554 processor with 30 physical cores operating at 3.3 GHz, paired with 42.0 GB of system memory and a 400 GB local SSD. Acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA A10 GPU with 24 GB of VRAM. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance and strong multi-core execution, alongside exceptional LLM inference speeds for small and medium models. However, memory bandwidth for large blocks remains average, and text generation for 70B models is limited by the 24 GB VRAM capacity. With an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this server is highly suited for medium-scale machine learning, parallel data processing, and 3D rendering workloads where dedicated CPU cores and GPU acceleration are required.
og_description: OVHcloud a10-45 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 30 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 42 GB RAM, a 400 GB SSD, and an NVIDIA A10 GPU with 24 GB VRAM, optimized for medium-scale machine learning and compute workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud a10-45 is a KVM server with 30 AMD EPYC cores, 42 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA A10 GPU, delivering top-tier single-core and LLM inference performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute platform paired with NVIDIA A10 acceleration for efficient medium-scale machine learning and parallel processing.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9554 processor with 30 physical cores at 3.3 GHz
NVIDIA A10 GPU with 24 GB VRAM for hardware-accelerated workloads
42 GB system memory and 400 GB local SSD storage
Top-tier LLM inference performance for models up to 7B parameters
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Ideal for medium-scale machine learning, 3D rendering, and parallel computing
